---
title: "Reproducibility"
---

Tools that make an analysis trail auditable from raw data to figure. The shortlist is intentionally narrow — the goal is to pick a stack and use it, not to compare twenty options.

## Authoring

::: {.resource-entry}
### [Quarto](https://quarto.org/)
License: OSS · MIT  ·  Maintainer: Posit

The successor to R Markdown. Renders the same document to HTML, PDF, Word, slides, books, and websites. Native multi-language support (R, Python, Julia, Observable). This site is built with it.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [R Markdown](https://rmarkdown.rstudio.com/)
License: OSS · GPL-3.0  ·  Maintainer: Posit

Still actively maintained. New projects should default to Quarto; existing R Markdown projects do not need to migrate urgently.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [workflowr](https://workflowr.github.io/workflowr/)
License: OSS · MIT  ·  Maintainer: John Blischak

Builds a reproducible-by-construction website from an R-Markdown analysis directory: every page records a git commit, RNG seed, and session info. CTIR's [`reflowR`](https://github.com/CTTIR/reflowR) provides custom themes for it.
:::

## Pipelines

::: {.resource-entry}
### [targets](https://docs.ropensci.org/targets/)
License: OSS · MIT  ·  Maintainer: Will Landau / rOpenSci

See the [R ecosystem](r-ecosystem.qmd) page. Default choice for non-trivial R pipelines.
:::

## Environment and dependencies

::: {.resource-entry}
### [renv](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/)
License: OSS · MIT  ·  Maintainer: Posit

R project lockfile. Records exact package versions; restores them on another machine.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [Rocker](https://rocker-project.org/)
License: OSS · GPL-2.0 (images) / project content varies  ·  Maintainer: Rocker Project

Curated Docker images for R, including Bioconductor-flavoured images and `tidyverse`-preinstalled stacks. The path of least resistance to a reproducible container.
:::

## Archival and sharing

::: {.resource-entry}
### [Zenodo](https://zenodo.org/)
License: PD (service); content varies  ·  Maintainer: CERN

DOI minting for code, data, and supplementary material. Integrates directly with GitHub for one-click archival of releases.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [OSF — Open Science Framework](https://osf.io/)
License: OSS · Apache-2.0 (platform); content varies  ·  Maintainer: Center for Open Science

Project-centric hub for protocols, preregistration, code, and data. Useful for the ancillary materials that don't fit on Zenodo.
:::

::: {.resource-entry}
### [Binder](https://mybinder.org/)
License: OSS · BSD-3-Clause  ·  Maintainer: Project Jupyter

Builds an executable container from a public repo and serves it as a notebook in the browser. Lowest-friction "click to run my analysis" link for reviewers.
:::
